content.problemStatement:
You are given 222 integers AAA and BBB.

Find any integer XXX (0ŌēżX<2300 \leq X \lt 2^{30}0ŌēżX<230) such that ŌłŻ(AŌŖĢX)ŌłÆ(BŌŖĢX)ŌłŻ| (A \oplus X) - (B \oplus X) |ŌłŻ(AŌŖĢX)ŌłÆ(BŌŖĢX)ŌłŻ is **minimized**.

Here, ŌŖĢ\oplusŌŖĢ denotes the bitwise XOR operation, and ŌłŻAŌłŻ|A|ŌłŻAŌłŻ denotes the absolute value of AAA.

If there are multiple possible XXX that minimize the given expression, you may print any of them.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case will contain two space-separated integers AAA and BBB.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the integer XXX (0ŌēżX<2300 \leq X \lt 2^{30}0ŌēżX<230) that minimizes the given expression.

If there are multiple possible answers, you may print any of them.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: ŌłŻ(4ŌŖĢ1)ŌłÆ(4ŌŖĢ2)ŌłŻ=ŌłŻ5ŌłÆ6ŌłŻ=1| (4 \oplus 1) - (4 \oplus 2) | = | 5 - 6 | = 1 ŌłŻ(4ŌŖĢ1)ŌłÆ(4ŌŖĢ2)ŌłŻ=ŌłŻ5ŌłÆ6ŌłŻ=1.
It can be shown that difference cannot become less than 111.
Note that there are multiple possible answers here: for example X=0X = 0X=0 or X=3X = 3X=3 would also be acceptable.
Test case 222: ŌłŻ(3ŌŖĢ0)ŌłÆ(3ŌŖĢ0)ŌłŻ=ŌłŻ3ŌłÆ3ŌłŻ=0| (3 \oplus 0) - (3 \oplus 0) | = | 3 - 3 | = 0 ŌłŻ(3ŌŖĢ0)ŌłÆ(3ŌŖĢ0)ŌłŻ=ŌłŻ3ŌłÆ3ŌłŻ=0, which is the minimum possible difference.
Test case 333: ŌłŻ(5ŌŖĢ11)ŌłÆ(1ŌŖĢ11)ŌłŻ=ŌłŻ14ŌłÆ10ŌłŻ=4| (5 \oplus 11) - (1 \oplus 11) | = | 14 - 10 | = 4 ŌłŻ(5ŌŖĢ11)ŌłÆ(1ŌŖĢ11)ŌłŻ=ŌłŻ14ŌłÆ10ŌłŻ=4, which is the best we can do.
Test case 444: ŌłŻ(0ŌŖĢ8)ŌłÆ(15ŌŖĢ8)ŌłŻ=ŌłŻ8ŌłÆ7ŌłŻ=1| (0 \oplus 8) - (15\oplus 8) | = |8 - 7| = 1ŌłŻ(0ŌŖĢ8)ŌłÆ(15ŌŖĢ8)ŌłŻ=ŌłŻ8ŌłÆ7ŌłŻ=1.
